key_insight: BASE formalizes only one candidate answer per problem and edits that Lean statement for the remaining candidates, reducing verification cost while improving answer selection.
engineering_relevance: This makes Lean-based test-time scaling more practical by cutting repeated autoformalization calls, which can lower latency and compute in math-reasoning pipelines.
method: Formalize one base candidate answer in Lean for each problem.
Train LEANSCRIBE to locate the answer expression and produce an edit function.
Reuse the edit to derive the other K-1 Lean statements instead of re-formalizing each one.
evidence_signal: Across 12 dataset-solver configurations on four benchmarks and three solvers, the method is reported to improve selection accuracy while cutting autoformalizer calls by about 5x at K=8.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliably localizing and editing the answer expression, so complex formalizations or brittle rewrites could affect robustness.
paper_title: Formalize Once, Edit the Rest: Efficient Lean-Based Answer Selection for Math Reasoning
